If cash flow enables you and your investors and an entrepreneur to weather anything and everything and it will come, any major shift in the housing market, economic turn downs and worse can be covered if you have a sufficient amount of cash flow. So you have your NOI, your debt, the bank is your partner, it's your best partner because he's not going to let you over leverage or over borrow on the property. He's going to make sure there's cash flow left over. In fact, before you get all your cash flow, the bank is probably going to require that you actually hold some money in reserve to fix the property. This is called your debt coverage ratio. This calculation is more important than the appraisal. It's more important than the price you paid. Your credit or percentage of down payment. The debt coverage ratio of 115 to 120, maybe 125 suggests the amount of the principal and interest that will be covered by the NOI. If the property's debt is 100,000 a year, the NOI needs to be 120,000 to 125,000 to meet the bank's requirements. By doing this and following the bank's requirements of the DCR, this will allow you, your investors and your property to have sufficient cash flow.
